subject: Statistics
title: Practical vs. Statistical Significance
description: A quick, practical guide to Practical vs. Statistical Significance.

hook.text: A study of 10 million finds that coffee drinkers live 0.3 days longer. p < 0.00001. Should you care?

buildup.text: Statistical significance means the effect is unlikely due to chance. Practical significance means the effect matters.

discovery.text: With huge samples, trivially small effects become statistically significant. Big data amplifies tiny differences.

twist.text: A 0.3-day lifespan gain won't change any health recommendation. But the p-value screams 'significant!'

climax.text: Always ask two questions: is it real, and does it matter? Statistics answers the first. Judgment answers the second.

punchline.text: Real and meaningful are two different things.

quiz.question: What's the difference between statistical and practical significance?
options: Statistical significance means the effect is real; practical significance means it's large enough to matter
Practical significance only applies to business
